In the opinion of Peck, Shaffer & Williams, a division of Dinsmore & Shohl LLP and Gonzalez, Saggio & Harlan LLP, Co-
Bond Counsel with respect to the Series 2015 Bonds, based upon an analysis of existing laws, regulations, rulings and court 
decisions, and assuming continuing compliance with certain covenants made by the City of Cincinnati, Ohio (i) interest 
on the Series 2015A Bonds will be excludible from gross income of the holders thereof for purposes of federal income taxation 
and interest on the Series 2015A Bonds will not be a specific item of tax preference for purposes of the federal alternative 
minimum tax imposed on individuals and corporations, (ii) interest on the Series 2015B Bonds and the Series 2015C 
Refunding Bonds will not be excludible from gross income for federal income tax purposes, and (iii) interest on and any profit 
made on the sale, exchange, or other disposition of, the Series 2015 Bonds, are exempt from the Ohio personal income tax, the 
net income base of the Ohio corporate franchise tax, the Ohio commercial activity tax, and municipal, school district, and 
joint economic development district income taxes in Ohio, all subject to the qualifications described herein under the heading 
“LEGAL MATTERS –  Tax Status.”

The $122,530,000 City of Cincinnati, Ohio Unlimited Tax Various Purpose General Obligation Improvement and Refunding 
Bonds, Series 2015A (the “Series 2015A Bonds”), $3,450,000 Unlimited Tax Energy Conservation General Obligation Bonds, Series 
2015B (Federally Taxable - Qualified Energy Conservation Bond – Direct Payment to the Issuer) (the “Series 2015B Bonds”), and 
$14,690,000 Unlimited Tax Various Purpose General Obligation Refunding Bonds, Series 2015C (Federally Taxable) (the “Series 
2015C Refunding Bonds” and collectively with the Series 2015A Bonds and the Series 2015B Bonds, the “Series 2015 Bonds”) 
of the City of Cincinnati, Ohio (the “City”) are issuable as fully registered Series 2015 Bonds and, when issued, will be initially 
registered in the name of Cede & Co., as nominee for The Depository Trust Company, New York, New York (“DTC”).  Purchases of 
beneficial interests in the Series 2015 Bonds will be made in book-entry-only form.  Purchasers of beneficial interests (“Beneficial 
Owners”) will not receive certificates representing their interests in the Series 2015 Bonds.  So long as the Series 2015 Bonds are 
registered in the name of Cede & Co., as nominee of DTC, references herein to the owners shall mean Cede & Co. and shall not 
mean the Beneficial Owners of the Series 2015 Bonds.
Payment of the principal of and premium, if any, and interest on, the Series 2015 Bonds will be made directly to DTC or 
its nominee, Cede & Co., so long as DTC or Cede & Co. is the sole registered owner.  Disbursement of such payments to DTC’s 
Participants is the responsibility of DTC, and disbursement of such payments to the Beneficial Owners is the responsibility of 
DTC’s Participants and the Indirect Participants, as more fully described under “BOOK-ENTRY ONLY SYSTEM” and Appendix D 
hereto.
Interest on the Series 2015 Bonds will be payable semi-annually on June 1 and December 1 of each year, beginning 
December 1, 2015.  The Series 2015 Bonds will be issued in authorized denominations of $5,000 and integral multiples thereof.
The full faith and credit of the City are irrevocably pledged for the prompt payment of the principal of and interest on the 
Series 2015 Bonds.  See “SECURITY AND SOURCE OF PAYMENT” herein.  
The Series 2015 Bonds are subject to redemption prior to their stated maturity as described under “SECURITIES BEING 
OFFERED – Redemption Prior to Maturity” herein.
This cover page includes certain information for quick reference only.  It is not a summary of the bond issue. Investors 
should read the entire Official Statement to obtain information as a basis for making informed investment judgments.
The Series 2015 Bonds are offered when, as and if issued and accepted by the Underwriters identified herein, subject to prior 
sale and the approval of certain legal matters by Peck, Shaffer & Williams, a division of Dinsmore & Shohl LLP, Cincinnati, Ohio, 
and Gonzalez, Saggio & Harlan LLP, Cleveland, Ohio, Co-Bond Counsel, and certain other conditions.  Certain legal matters will 
be passed upon for the Underwriters by their counsel, Thompson Hine LLP, Cincinnati, Ohio and Forbes, Fields & Associates Co., 
L.P.A., Cleveland, Ohio.  Davenport & Company LLC has acted as Financial Advisor to the City in connection with the issuance of 
the Series 2015 Bonds.  It is expected that delivery of the Series 2015 Bonds will be made in book entry form to DTC in New York, 
New York on or about August 20, 2015.

REGARDING USE OF THIS OFFICIAL STATEMENT 
The information set forth herein has been obtained from the City and other sources that 
are believed to be reliable for purposes of this Official Statement.  This Official Statement 
contains, in part, estimates and matters of opinion that are not intended as statements of fact, and 
no representation is made as to the correctness of such estimates and opinions or that they will be 
realized.  The information and expressions of op inion contained herein are subject to change 
without notice, and neither the delivery of this Official Statement, nor any sale made hereunder, 
shall under any circumstances create any implication that there has been no change in the affairs 
of the City or in the information or opinions se t forth herein, since the date of this Official 
Statement. 
This Official Statement, which includes th e cover pages and the Appendices hereto, does 
not constitute an offer to sell or the solicitation of  an offer to buy, nor shall there be any sale of 
the Series 2015 Bonds by any person in any jurisdiction in which it is unlawful for such person 
to make such offer, solicitation or sale.  No d ealer, broker, salesperson, or other person has been 
authorized to give any information or to make any representations other than those contained in 
this Official Statement in connection with the offering of the Series 2015 Bonds, and, if given or 
made, such other information or representa tion must not be relied upon as having been 
authorized by the City. 
This Official Statement contains statements which, to the extent they are not recitations 
of historical fact, constitute “f orward-looking statements.”  In th is respect the words “estimate,” 
“project,” “anticipate,” “expect,” “intend,” “b elieve” and similar expressions are intended to 
identify forward-looking statements.  A number of important factors affecting the City's financial 
results could cause actual results to differ materi ally from those stated in the forward-looking 
statements. 
The information and expressions of opinion here in are subject to ch ange without notice, 
and neither the delivery of this  Official Statement nor any sale  made hereunder shall, under any 
circumstances, give rise to an implication that th ere has been no change in the affairs of the City 
since the date hereof. 
The Underwriters have reviewed the informati on in this Official Statement in accordance 
with, and as part of, their res ponsibility to investors under the fe deral securities laws as applied 
to the facts and circumstances of this transa ction, but the Underwrite rs do not guarantee the 
accuracy or completeness of such information.

BOND ISSUE SUMMARY FOR SERIES 2015A BONDS 
The information contained in this Bond Issue Summary is qualified in its entirety by 
the entire Official Statement, which should be reviewed in its entirety by potential investors. 
Issuer: City of Cincinnati, Ohio 
Issue: $122,530,000 Unlimited Tax Various Purpose General Obligation 
Improvement and Refunding Bonds, Series 2015A (the “Series 2015A 
Bonds) 
Dated Date: Date of Delivery 
Interest 
Payment Dates: 
Each June 1 and December 1, beginning December 1, 2015 
Principal 
Payment Dates: 
December 1, 2015 through December 1, 2040, inclusive 
Redemption: The Series 2015A Bonds maturing on or after December 1, 2025, are 
subject to redemption, by and at the sole option of the City at any time 
on or after June 1, 2025, in whole or in part, in such order of maturity as 
may be determined by the City, at a redemption price equal to 100% of 
the principal amount redeemed, plus accrued interest to the date fixed 
for redemption. 
Purpose: The Series 2015A Bond s are comprised of a new money portion (the 
“Series 2015A Improvement Bonds”) and a refunding portion (the “Series 
2015A Refunding Bonds”).   
(i) Proceeds derived from the sale  of the Series 2015A Improvement 
Bonds described herein will be used to make various 
improvements throughout the City including, but not limited to: 
(a) widening, opening, extending, realigning, grading, repaving, 
resurfacing, constr ucting sewers and dr ains or otherwise 
rehabilitating and improving st reets, roads, thoroughfares, 
avenues, expressways. sidewalks, plazas and other public 
ways; purchasing and/or in stalling street lights and 
equipment and boulevard lights, (collectively, the “Street 
Improvement Project”);  
(b)  public building improvements including acquiring real estate 
or interests in same, constructing, rehabilitating and 
equipping public buildings and ot her structures (collectively, 
the “Buildings Improvement Project”);  
(c)  purchasing motor vehicles, acquiring and improving City-
wide communication system components and related 
improvements, computer related system upgrades and 
components and other necessary equipment;  
iv 
 
(d)  park improvements includi ng acquiring real estate or 
interests in same, for park s, parkways, playgrounds and 
recreation centers, improving a nd equipping such real estate 
and also rehabilitating existing parks, parkways, playgrounds 
and recreation centers;  
(e)  various improvements asso ciated with public building 
improvements including acquiring real estate or interests in 
same, constructing, rehabil itating and equipping public 
buildings and other structures (collectively, the “Linn Street 
Facility Project”);  
(f) paying a portion of the City’s share of the costs associated 
with the construction of a new parking garage in the 
Corryville Neighborhood Business District of Cincinnati, 
such bonds designated as Economic Development Revenue 
Bonds (VP3 Parking Garage) (the “Corryville Neighborhood 
Business District Project”); 
(g) acquiring, installing and se rvicing energy cost-savings 
equipment and energy management building improvements 
for the purpose of achieving en ergy cost reductions and cost 
savings within various public buildings (collectively, the 
“Energy Management Building Improvement Project”);  
(h) acquiring, installing and se rvicing energy cost-savings 
equipment and energy management building improvements 
for the purpose of achieving en ergy cost reductions and cost 
savings at the Duke Energy C onvention Center (collectively, 
the “Duke Energy Convention Center Project”);  
(i) refunding bond anticipation notes previously issued for the 
purpose of providing interim fina ncing for all or a portion of 
the improvements described in items (a) through (h) above; 
and 
(j) reimbursing the City for monies previously spent for 
improvements including, certa in urban redevelopment 
activities associated with the Streetcar Project including but 
not limited to, planning and desi gn work, streetcar vehicles, 
other rolling stock, tracks, electrification system, catenaries, 
equipment, maintenance and storage facilities, infrastructure 
improvements and refunding bond anticipation notes 
previously issued for the purpose of financing such 
improvements related to the Streetcar Project (collectively, 
the “Streetcar Project”);  
  
v 
 
(ii) Proceeds derived from the sale of the Series 2015A Refunding 
Bonds described herein will be used to refund various 
outstanding general obligation bond s of the City (the “Refunded 
Tax-Exempt Bonds”), as described in further detail herein under 
“SECURITIES BEING OFFERED – Authority for Issuance – 
Series 2015A Bonds” and “USE OF BOND PROCEEDS – Use 
of Proceeds of the Series 2015A Bonds - Series 2015A 
Refunding Bonds.” 
Security and 
Sources of 
Payment: 
General.  The Series 2015A Bonds will be unvoted general obligations 
of the City and will contain a pledge  of the full faith and credit of the 
City for the payment of the principal of and interest on the Series 2015A 
Bonds when due.  The City maintains the ability to le vy an ad valorem 
tax on all real and personal property in the City subject to ad valorem 
taxation by the City, without limitati on as to rate and amount, unless 
paid from other sources as they are appropriated and available.   
Corryville Neighborhood Bu siness District Project.   With respect to a 
portion of the Series 2015A Improvement Bonds, the proceeds of which 
have been allocated to the fina ncing of the Corryville Neighborhood 
Business District Project in th e amount of $6,000,000 (pursuant to 
Ordinance Number 0309-2013), the City may also covenant to annually 
appropriate other taxes and revenues,  other than ad valorem property 
taxes, as specified by the City, in  amounts necessary for the prompt 
payment of the principal of and interest on such  portion of the Series 
2015A Improvement Bonds.   
Energy Management Building Improvement Project.  With respect to a 
portion of the Series 2015A Improvement Bonds, the proceeds of which 
have been allocated to the financing of the Energy Management 
Building Improvement Project in the amount of $3,175,000 (pursuant to 
Ordinance Number 0009-2014, as am ended by Ordinance Number 
0160-2015), the City may also covenant to annually appropriate other 
taxes and revenues, other than ad valorem property taxes, including 
monies related to energy cost savings, as provided in such Ordinance, as 
specified by the City, for the prompt payment of principal of and 
interest on such portion of the Series 2015A Improvement Bonds. 
Duke Energy Convention Center Project.   With respect to a portion of 
the Series 2015A Improvement Bonds, the proceeds of which have been 
allocated to the financing of th e Duke Energy Convention Center 
Project in the amount of $1,160,000 ( pursuant to Ordinance Number 
0290-2014), the City may also covenant to annually appropriate other 
taxes and revenues, other than ad valorem property taxes, including 
monies related to energy cost savings, as provided in such Ordinance, as 
specified by the City, for the prompt payment of principal of and 
interest on such portion of the Series 2015A Improvement Bonds. 
  
vi 
Municipal Income Tax Pledge.   With respect to a portion of the Series 
2015A Improvement Bonds, the proceeds of which have been allocated 
to the financing of (i) a portion of the Street Improveme nt Project in the 
amount of $4,000,000 (pursuant to Or dinance Number 0125-2014), (ii) 
a portion of the Buildings Improvement Project in the amount of 
$3,000,000 (pursuant to Ordinance Nu mber 0127-2014) and (iii) the 
Linn Street Facility Project in  the amount of $7,500,000 (pursuant to 
Ordinance Number 0015-2015), the City covenants to annually 
appropriate municipal income taxes in amounts necessary for the 
prompt payment of the principal of and interest on such portion of the 
Series 2015A Improvement Bonds. 
With respect to a portion of the Series 2015A Refunding Bonds, the 
proceeds of which have been alloca ted to the refunding of a portion of 
(i) the Series 2006B Bonds (as define d herein) (pursuant to Ordinance 
Numbers 0081-2005, 0217-2005, 0047-2006 and 0048-2005), (ii) the 
Series 2007B Bonds (as defined herein) (pursuant to Ordinance 
Numbers 0203-2007, 0204-2007 and 0076-2007), (iii) the Series 2008B 
Bonds (as defined herein) (pursuant to Ordinance Numbers 0048-2008 
and 0046-2008), and (iv) the Series 2009B Bonds (as defined herein) 
(pursuant to Ordinance Numbers 0047-2009, 0044-2009 and 0070-
2009), the City covenants to annually appropriate municipal income 
taxes in amounts necessary for the pr ompt payment of the principal of 
and interest on such portion of the Series 2015A Refunding Bonds. 
Pledge of Other City Revenues.   With respect to a portion of the Series 
2015A Refunding Bonds, the proceeds of  which have been  allocated to 
the refunding of a portion of the Series 2005C Bonds (as defined herein) 
in the amount of $26,670,000 (pursu ant to Ordinance Numbers 0021-
2000 and 0191-2005), the City may al so covenant to annually 
appropriate other sources of reve nue in amounts necessary for the 
prompt payment of the principal of and interest on such portion of the 
Series 2015A Refunding Bonds. 
Credit Ratings: Moody's Investors Service (“Moody’s”)  and Standard & Poor's Ratings 
Services, a division of The McGraw-H ill Companies, Inc. (“Standard & 
Poor's”), have rated the Series 2015A Bonds “Aa2” (stable outlook) and 
“AA-” (stable outlook), respectively. 
Tax Status: In the opinion of Co-Bond Counsel, under existing law and assuming 
compliance with certain covenants, interest on the Series 2015A Bonds 
is excludible from gross income fo r federal income tax purposes, is not 
treated as a specific item of tax preference for purposes of the 
alternative minimum income ta x imposed on individuals and 
corporations under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as amended. The 
City has not designated the Seri es 2015A Bonds as “qualified tax 
exempt obligations” within the me aning of Section 265(b)(3) of the 
Code.  Interest on the Series 2015A Bonds may be subj ect to certain 
federal income taxes imposed on cer tain corporations, and certain 
taxpayers may have certain othe r adverse federal income tax  
vii 
consequences as a result of owning the Series 2015A Bonds.  The Series 
2015A Bonds, the transfer thereof, a nd the income therefrom, including 
any profit made on the sale thereof, exchange or other disposition of the 
Series 2015A Bonds are exempt from the Ohio personal income tax, the 
net income base of the Ohio corporate franchise tax, the Ohio 
commercial activity tax, and municipa l, school district, and joint 
economic development district income taxes in Ohio, all subject to the 
qualifications described herein.  See “LEGAL MATTERS – Tax Status- 
Tax-Exempt Series 2015 Bonds” herein. 
Legal Opinion: Peck, Shaffer & Williams, a divi sion of Dinsmore & Shohl LLP, 
Cincinnati, Ohio and Gonzalez, Saggio & Harlan LLP, Cleveland, Ohio

U.S. Bank National Association, Cincinnati, Ohio 
Book-Entry 
System: 
The Series 2015A Bonds are being issu ed as fully registered Series 
2015A Bonds in book entry form only and book-entry interests therein 
will be available for purchase in amounts of $5,000 and integral 
multiples thereof.  Owners of book entry interests will not receive 
physical delivery of bond certificates.  The Depository Trust Company 
(“DTC”) or its nominee will receive all payments with respect to the 
Series 2015A Bonds from the Paying Agent and Registrar.  DTC is 
required by its rules and procedures  to remit such payments to its 
participants for subsequent disbur sement to owners of the book entry 
interests. 
Delivery and 
Payment: 
It is expected that delivery of the Series 2015A Bonds in definitive form 
will be made on or about August 20, 2015.  The Series 2015A Bonds 
will be released to the Underwriters against payment in federal funds.

BOND ISSUE SUMMARY FOR SERIES 2015B BONDS 
The information contained in this Bond Issue Summary is qualified in its entirety by 
the entire Official Statement, which should be reviewed in its entirety by potential investors. 
Issuer: City of Cincinnati, Ohio 
Issue: $3,450,000 Unlimited Tax Energy Conservation General Obligation 
Bonds, Series 2015B (Federally Taxable - Qualified Energy 
Conservation Bond – Direct Payment to the Issuer) (the “Series 2015B 
Bonds”) 
Dated Date: Date of Delivery 
Interest 
Payment Dates: 
Each June 1 and December 1, beginning December 1, 2015 
Principal 
Payment Date: 
December 1, 2035 
Redemption: Ordinary Optional Redemption.  The Series 2015B Bonds maturing on 
or after December 1, 2025 are subject to redemption, by and at the sole 
option of the City, in w hole or in part (in incr ements of $5,000, in such 
order of maturity as may be dete rmined by the City ) on any Business 
Day (as defined herein), at the Make-Whole Redemption Price (as 
defined herein), all as further provided herein under “SECURITIES 
BEING OFFERED – Redemption Prior to Maturity - Provisions with 
respect to the Series 2015B Bonds as Qualified Energy Conservation 
Bonds”). 
Extraordinary Optional Redemption.   The Series 2015B Bonds are 
subject to extraordinary optional redemption prior to maturity, by and at 
the sole option of the City, in whole or in part (as selected by the City) 
upon the occurrence of an event resu lting in the reduction or cessation 
of the Qualified Energy Conservation Payments (as defined herein) 
related to the Series 2015B Bonds on any Business Day at the Make-
Whole Redemption Price, with any reduction being measured against 
the Qualified Energy Conservation Paym ents in effect at the time of 
issuance of the Series 2015B Bonds, other than a reduction or cessation 
that is the result of an act or omission by the City relative to the 
requirements of the Internal Reve nue Code of 1986, as amended (the 
“Code”) and regulations thereunder (as further provided herein under 
“SECURITIES BEING OFFERED – Re demption Prior to Maturity - 
Provisions with respect to the Seri es 2015B Bonds as Qualified Energy 
Conservation Bonds”). 
 
 
  
ix 
Extraordinary Mandatory Redemption.   The Series 2015B Bonds are 
subject to extraordinary mandatory re demption, in whole or in part at a 
redemption price of 100% of the princi pal amount to be redeemed, plus 
accrued interest to the redemption date, if the City fails to expend all of 
the available project proceeds (as de fined in Section 54A of the Code) 
of the Series 2015B Bonds within the three-year period beginning on the 
issue date of the Series 2015B Bonds (or, if an extension of the 
expenditure period has been received by the City from the Secretary of 
the U.S. Treasury, at the close of th e extended period) within ninety 90 
days of the end of such period to th e extent necessary, in the opinion of 
nationally recognized bond counsel rendered at the request  of the City 
or pursuant to a final determination by the Internal Revenue Service or 
court of competent jurisdiction in the United States, to preserve the 
federal tax status of the Series  2015B Bonds as Qualified Energy 
Conservation Bonds. 
Purpose: Proceeds derived from the sale of  the Series 2015B Bonds described 
herein will be used to refund bond anticipation notes previously issued for 
the purpose of providing interim financing for the purpose of acquiring, 
installing and servicin g energy cost-savings equipment and energy 
management building improvements for the purpose of achieving energy 
cost reductions and cost savings within various public buildings 
(collectively, the “Qualified Energy Conservation Project”).  
Security and 
Sources of 
Payment: 
General.  The Series 2015B Bonds will be unvoted general obligations 
of the City and will contain a pledge  of the full faith and credit of the 
City for the payment of the principal of and interest on the Series 2015B 
Bonds when due.  The City maintains the ability to le vy an ad valorem 
tax on all real and personal property in the City subject to ad valorem 
taxation by the City, without limitati on as to rate and amount, unless 
paid from other sources as they are appropriated and available.   
The Qualified Energy Conservation Payments that the City expects to 
receive with respect to the Series 2015B Bonds are not pledged for the 
payment of principal of, interest on or any premium on any of the Series 
2015B Bonds. 
Pledge of Other City Revenues.   The City may also covenant to 
annually appropriate other taxes and revenues, other than ad valorem 
property taxes, including monies re lated to energy cost savings, as 
provided in Ordinance Numbers 0009-2014, as amended by Ordinance 
Number 0160-2015, as specified by the City, for the prompt payment of 
principal of and interest on the Series 2015B Bonds. (See 
“SECURITIES BEING OFFERED - Seri es 2015B Bonds as Qualified 
Energy Conservation Bonds” herein.)  
Credit Ratings: Moody's and Standard & Poor's have  rated the Series 2015B Bonds 
“Aa2” (stable outlook) and “AA-” (stable outlook), respectively. 
   
x 
Tax Status: In the opinion of Co-Bond Counsel, under existing law and assuming 
compliance with certain covenants, interest on the Series 2015B Bonds 
is not excludible from gross income  for federal income tax purposes.  
The Series 2015B Bonds, the transf er thereof, and the income 
therefrom, including any profit made on the sale thereof, exchange or 
other disposition of the Series 2015B Bonds are exempt from the Ohio 
personal income tax, the net income  base of the Ohio corporate 
franchise tax, the Ohio commercial ac tivity tax, and municipal, school 
district, and joint economic development district income taxes in Ohio, 
all subject to the qualifications described herein.  See “LEGAL 
MATTERS – Tax Status– Tax-Exempt Series 2015 Bonds” herein.

BOND ISSUE SUMMARY FOR SERIES 2015C REFUNDING BONDS 
The information contained in this Bond Issue Summary is qualified in its entirety by 
the entire Official Statement, which should be reviewed in its entirety by potential investors. 
Issuer: City of Cincinnati, Ohio 
Issue: $14,690,000 Unlimited Tax Various Purpose General Obligation 
Refunding Bonds, Series 2015C (Federally Taxable) (the “Series 2015C 
Refunding Bonds”) 
Dated Date: Date of Delivery 
Interest Payment 
Dates: 
Each June 1 and December 1, beginning December 1, 2015 
Principal Payment 
Dates: 
December 1, 2015 through December 1, 2026, inclusive 
Redemption: The Series 2015C Refunding Bonds ar e not subject to redemption prior 
to their stated maturity. 
Purpose: Proceeds derived from the sale of  the Series 2015 C Refunding Bonds 
described herein will be used to refund various ou tstanding general 
obligation bonds of the City (the  “Taxable Refunded Tax-Exempt 
Bonds”), as described in furthe r detail herein under “SECURITIES 
BEING OFFERED – Authority for I ssuance – Series 2015C Refunding 
Bonds” and “USE OF BOND PROC EEDS – Use of Proceeds of the 
Series 2015C Refunding Bonds.” 
Security and 
Sources of 
Payment: 
General.  The Series 2015C Refunding Bonds will be unvoted general 
obligations of the City and will co ntain a pledge of the full faith and 
credit of the City for the payment of the principal of and interest on the 
Series 2015C Refunding Bonds when  due.  The City maintains the 
ability to levy an ad valorem tax on all real and personal property in the 
City subject to ad valo rem taxation by the City, without limitation as to 
rate and amount, unless paid from other sources as they are appropriated 
and available.  
Pledge of Other City Revenues.   With respect to a portion of the Series 
2015C Refunding Bonds, the proceeds of  which have been allocated to 
the refunding of a portion of (i) th e Series 2004B Bonds (as defined 
herein) in the amount of $4,615,000 ( pursuant to Ordinance Number 
0108-2004), and (ii) the Series 2006C B onds (as defined herein) in the 
amount of $1,930,000 (pursuant to Ordinance Number 0286-2005), the 
City may also covenant to annua lly appropriate other Revenues (as 
defined in each respective authorizing ordinance), other than ad valorem 
property taxes, as specified by the City, in amounts necessary for the 
prompt payment of the principal of and interest on such portion of the 
Series 2015C Refunding Bonds. 
Credit Ratings: Moody's and Standard & Poor's have  rated the Series 2015C Refunding 
Bonds “Aa2” (stable outlook) and “AA-” (stable outlook), respectively.

GENERAL COMMENTS 
THESE COMMENTS ARE NOT A SU MMARY OF THIS OFFICIAL 
STATEMENT.  THEY ARE A BRIEF DESCRIPTION OF AND GUIDE TO, AND ARE 
QUALIFIED BY, MORE COMPLETE AND DETAILED INFORMATION CONTAINED 
IN THE ENTIRE OFFICIAL STATEMEN T, INCLUDING THE COVER PAGE AND 
APPENDICES HERETO. A FULL REVIEW SHOULD BE MADE OF THE ENTIRE 
OFFICIAL STATEMENT.  THE OFFERI NG OF THE SERIES 2015 BONDS TO 
POTENTIAL INVESTORS IS MADE ONLY BY MEANS OF THE ENTIRE OFFICIAL 
STATEMENT. 
 
The purpose of this Official Statement,  which includes the cover pages and the 
appendices hereto, is to provide certain information with respect to the issuance of three series of 
General Obligation Bonds of the City of Cincinnati, Ohio (the “City”): 
(a) $122,530,000 Unlimited Tax Various Pu rpose General Obligation 
Improvement and Refunding Bonds, Series 2015A (the “Series 2015A Bonds”);  
(b) $3,450,000 Unlimited Tax Energy Conservation General Obligation 
Bonds, Series 2015B (Federally Taxable - Qualified Energy Conservation Bond – Direct 
Payment to the Issuer) (Additional Municipa l Income Tax Pledge) (the “Series 2015B 
Bonds”); and 
(c) $14,690,000 Unlimited Tax Various Purpose General Obligation 
Refunding Bonds, Series 2015C (Federally Ta xable) (the “Series 2015C Refunding 
Bonds”). 
The Series 2015A Bonds, the Series 2015B Bonds, and the Series 2015C Refunding 
Bonds are referred to herein collectively as the “Series 2015 Bonds” in sections with information 
common to all Series.  The Series 2015A Bonds are referred to herein as the “Tax-Exempt Series 
2015 Bonds” in sections regarding tax matters.  The Series 2015B Bonds and the Series 2015C 
Refunding Bonds are referred to he rein together as the “Taxable  Series 2015 Bonds” in sections 
regarding tax matters. 
Disclosure Information 
This Official Statement speaks only as of its date, and the information contained herein is 
subject to change.  This Official Statement and continuing disclosure information of the City are 
intended to be made available through one or more repositori es.  Copies of the basic 
documentation relating to the Series 2015 Bonds, including the authorizing ordinances, are 
available from the City.  The City has deemed this Official Statement, dated August 6, 2015, to 
be final for the purposes of Securities and Exchange Commission Rule 15c2-12(b)(3).  
2 
The Issuer 
The Series 2015 Bonds are being issued by the City of Cincinnati, a political subdivision 
of the State of Ohio.  The City, which has a population of 296,943 (based on U.S. Census Bureau 
data for 2010), is the count y seat of Hamilton County in  southwestern Ohio (see Appendix A  
hereto). 
Offering and Delivery of the Series 2015 Bonds 
The Series 2015 Bonds are offered when, as and if issued and accepted by the 
Underwriters subject to prior sale and the approval of certain legal matters by Peck, Shaffer & 
Williams, a division of Dinsmore & Shohl LLP and Gonzalez, Saggio & Harlan LLP.  The 
Series 2015 Bonds will be delivered against payment in federal funds on or about August 20, 
2015. 
Security and Sources of Payment 
General.  The Series 2015 Bonds are unvoted general obligation debt of the City.  The 
security for the Series 2015 Bonds is the City's ability to levy an ad valorem tax on all real and 
personal property in the City s ubject to ad valorem taxation by the City, without limitation as to 
rate and amount, unless paid from other sources as  they are appropriated and available.  (See 
“SECURITY AND SOURCE OF PAYMENT” herein.) 
Additional Sources of Payment.  The City covenants to annually appropriate municipal 
income taxes in amounts necessary for the prompt payment of the principal of and interest on a 
portion of the Series 2015A Bonds.  The City ma y also covenant to annually appropriate other 
sources of revenue in amounts nece ssary for the prompt payment of the principal of and interest 
on certain other portions of the Series 2015A Bonds, the Series 2015B Bonds and the Series 
2015C Refunding Bonds.  See “SECURITY AND SOURCE OF PAYMENT – Additional 
Sources of Payment” herein. 
Tax Status 
Federal Tax Status.   
Tax Exempt Series 2015 Bonds.  Under the laws, regulations , rulings, and judicial 
decisions in effect as of the date hereof, in terest on the Tax-Exempt Series 2015 Bonds is 
excludible from gross income for federal in come tax purposes, pursu ant to the Internal 
Revenue Code of 1986, as amended (the “Cod e”). Furthermore, interest on the Series 
2015 Bonds will not be treated as a specif ic item of tax preference, under Section 
57(a)(5) of the Code, in computing the al ternative minimum tax for individuals and 
corporations.  In rendering the opinions in this paragraph, Co-Bond Counsel has assumed 
continuing compliance with certain covenants designed to meet the requirements of 
Section 103 of the Code.  Co-Bond Counsel ex presses no other opinion as to the federal 
tax consequences of purchasing, holding, or  disposing of the Ta x-Exempt Series 2015 
Bonds.  The City has not designated the Se ries 2015 Bonds as “qualified tax-exempt 
obligations” with respect to certain financia l institutions under Section 265 of the Code.  
See “LEGAL MATTERS – Tax Status– Tax-Exempt Series 2015 Bonds” herein.  
3 
Taxable Series 2015 Bonds.   Under the laws, regulati ons, rulings, and judicial 
decisions in effect as of the date hereof, interest on the Taxable Series 2015 Bonds is not 
excludible from gross income for federal income tax purposes, pursuant to the Code.  See 
“LEGAL MATTERS – Tax Status– Taxable Series 2015 Bonds” herein. 
Ohio Tax Exemption .  Under the laws, regulations, rulin gs, and judicial decisions in 
effect as of the date hereof, interest on and any profit made on the sale, exchange, or other 
disposition of, the Series 2015 Bonds, are exempt  from the Ohio personal income tax, the net 
income base of the Ohio corporate franchise tax, the Ohio commercial activity tax, and 
municipal, school district, and joint economic de velopment district income taxes in Ohio, all 
subject to the qualifications described herein. 
Forms of Opinions.  See Appendix C-1 hereto for the form of the opinion that Co-Bond 
Counsel proposes to deliver in  connection with the Tax-Ex empt Series 2015 Bonds and 
Appendix C-2 hereto for the form of the opinion that Co-Bond Counsel proposes to deliver in 
connection with the Taxable Series 2015 Bonds.

SECURITIES BEING OFFERED 
Authority for Issuance 
Authority for the City to issue bonds eman ates from Articles VI II and XVIII of the 
Constitution of the State of Ohio. Statutory provis ions are contained in the Ohio Revised Code, 
Section 133, Uniform Securities Act.  The Seri es 2015 Bonds, described herein, are lawfully 
issued in accordance with Article VIII, Section 4 of the City Charter of  the City which provides 
for the levy of ad valorem taxe s on all property in the City s ubject to ad valorem taxes for 
satisfying principal and interest requirements unless paid from other sources.

Consolidation into Single Issue of Bonds.   Pursuant to the prev ious ordinances adopted 
by the City Council, the Series 2015A Improveme nt Bonds, the Series 2015A Refunding Bonds, 
the Series 2015B Bonds, and the Series 2015C Refunding Bonds may be consolidated into a 
single issue of bonds enabling the City to consolidate all of the individual series listed above.  
Description of the Series 2015 Bonds 
The Series 2015 Bonds are general obligations  of the City.  The Series 2015 Bonds will 
pay interest semi-annually on the first day of June and December beginning December 1, 2015.  
Interest shall be computed on the basis of a 360- day year consisting of twelve 30-day months.  
Principal will be payable on the Series 2015 Bonds (either upon scheduled maturity or 
mandatory redemption) on the first day of December in the years and amounts as provided on the 
respective cover page hereto for each series, until final maturity.  The Series 2015 Bonds shall be 
issued as fully registered bonds. 
The bond registrar and paying ag ent (the “Paying Agent and Registrar”) for the Series 
2015 Bonds is U.S. Bank National Association, Cinc innati, Ohio.  Legal matters incident to the 
issuance of the Series 2015 Bonds and with regard to  the federal tax status of the interest thereon 
are subject to the approving legal opinion of Peck, Shaffer & Williams, a division of Dinsmore & 
Shohl LLP, Cincinnati, Ohio, a nd Gonzalez, Saggio & Harlan LLP, Cleveland, Ohio, Co-Bond 
Counsel.  The Underwriters for the Series 2015 Bonds are RBC Capital Markets, LLC, KeyBanc 
Capital Markets Inc., PNC Capital Market s LLC, Rice Financial Products Company, 
(collectively, the “Underwriters”).

Series 2015B Bonds as Qualified Energy Conservation Bonds 
The American Recovery and Reinvestment Act of 2009 (the “Recovery Act”) and the 
Hiring Incentives to Restore Employment Act enacted March 18, 2010 (the “HIRE Act”) permit 
state and local governments, such as the City, to obtain certain tax advantages when issuing 
certain taxable bonds, referred to as “Qualified Energy Conservation Bonds.”  Qualified Energy 
Conservation Bonds mean obligations permitted by the Recovery Act and the HIRE Act, (a) 
100% of the “available project proceeds” of which shall be used for one or more qualified 
conservation purposes, (b) which are issued by a state or local government, (c) which are 
irrevocably designated as Qualified Energy Conservation Bonds by the state or local government 
pursuant to Section 54D(a)(3) of the Code, (d) which meet all other requirements set forth in 
Sections 54A and 54D of the Code and, if an el ection is made, Section 6431(f) of the Code (and 
federal regulations applicable thereto) and IRS Notices 2010-35 and 2012-44, and (e) the issue 
price of the obligation does not have more than a de minimis amount of premium over the stated 
principal amount, as determined under rules sim ilar to the rules of S ection 1273(a)(3) of the 
Code governing original issue discount.  Adjustme nts to the arbitrage rules of Section 148 of the 
Code and to the federal guarantee provisions of S ection 149 of the Code ge nerally applicable to 
tax-exempt bonds are made for Qualified En ergy Conservation Bonds.  “Available project 
proceeds” is defined under Section 54A of the Code  as the sum of the (1) excess of the proceeds 
from the sale of an issue minus the issuance cost s financed by the issue, with such issuance costs 
not allowed to exceed two percent (2%) of the sale proceeds of the issue and (2) investment 
earnings on the excess computed in (1).  
Under the HIRE Act, an issuer of Qualif ied Energy Conservation Bonds may elect to 
receive “Qualified Energy Conservation Payments .” “Qualified Energy Conservation Payments” 
mean credit payments permitted pursuant to S ection 54A of the Code in conjunction with the 
irrevocable election of the issuer  to receive credit payments dire ctly from the U.S. Treasury, as 
provided in Section 6431 of the Code in lieu of the federal income tax credit that would 
otherwise be available to the holders of the related Qualified Energy Conservation Bonds.  The 
amount of a Qualified Energy Conservation Payment related to a Qualified Energy Conservation 
Bond is the lesser of (a) the amount of interest payable on the bond or (b) 70% of the amount of 
interest that would have been payable under the bond on its interest payment date if the interest 
was determined at the applicab le credit rate determined under Section 54A(b)(3) of the Code 
with respect to the bo nd (see statement below regarding recent reductions due to federal 
spending cuts).  The current app licable credit rate under Section 54A(b)(3) of the Code as of the 
date of this Official Statement is 4.65%.  To  receive Qualified Energy Conservation Payments, 
under the current procedures, an issuer such as the City will be required to file a tax form 
between 45 and 90 days before the applicable interest payment date for the related Qualified 
Energy Conservation Bond.  An issuer such as the City should expect to receive the Qualified 
Energy Conservation Payment contemporaneously w ith the applicable interest payment date.  
Depending on the timing of the filing, the Qua lified Energy Conservation Payment may be 
received before or after the corresponding interest payment date.  
Pursuant to the Budget Control Act of 2011 and the Sequestration Transparency Act of 
2012, cuts in certain categories of  federal spending were made for the fiscal year ending 
September 30, 2015, and may be made with respect to future fiscal years.  In connection 
therewith, the Internal Revenue Service has confirmed that refund payments related to 
refundable credits under Section 6431 of the Code, which includes Qualified Energy 
Conservation Payments, will be reduced by the fiscal year 2015 sequestration rate of 7.3 percent  
10 
for payments processed on or after October 1, 2014 and on or before September 30, 2015.  The 
sequestration reduction rate will be applied unle ss and until a law is enacted that cancels or 
otherwise impacts the sequester, at which time th e sequestration reduction rate is subject to 
change. 
Pursuant to Ordinance Number 0009-2014, as amended by Ordinance Number 0160-
2015, the City has irrevocably designated the Series 2015B Bonds as Qualified Energy 
Conservation Bonds and has irrevocably elec ted to receive Qualified Energy Conservation 
Payments under Section 6431(f) of the Code, in lieu of providing the hol ders of Series 2015B 
Bonds with a tax credit with respect to the Series 2015B Bonds.  Any Qualified Energy 
Conservation Payments related to the Series  2015B Bonds will be payable to the City.  THE 
HOLDERS OF THE SERIES 2015B BO NDS WILL NOT BE ENTITLED TO A TAX 
CREDIT.  The Qualified Energy Conservation Payments will be paid to the City only to the 
extent that the Series 2015B Bonds remain  Qualified Energy Conservation Bonds, which 
requires the City to comply with certain covenant s and to establish certain facts and expectations 
with respect to the Series 2015B Bonds, the use and investment of  proceeds thereof and the use 
of property financed thereby.  Also, Qualified Energy Conservation Payments may be subject to 
offset against certain amounts that may, for unrelat ed reasons, be owed by the City to an agency 
of the United States of America.  See also “TAX TREATMENT” herein.  The Qualified Energy 
Conservation Payments that the City expects to receive with respect to the Series 2015B Bonds 
are not pledged for the payment of  principal of, interest on or a ny premium on any of the Series 
2015B Bonds. 
Principal 
The Series 2015 Bonds will be issued in de nominations of $5,000 each or any integral 
multiple thereof.  Principal of the Series 2015 Bonds is payable, without deduction for exchange, 
collection, or service charges on each principal payment date to the person whose name appears 
on the bond registration records on the 15 th day of the calendar month preceding the principal 
payment date as the registered holder ther eof (initially, Cede & Co., as nominee for The 
Depository Trust Company (“DTC”)).  Interest th ereon shall be paid, without deduction for any 
exchange, collection, or service charges, on each interest payment date to the person whose name 
appears on the bond registration records on the 15 th day of the calendar month preceding the 
interest payment date as the re gistered holder thereof (initia lly, Cede & Co., as nominee for 
DTC).

Provisions with respect to the Series 2015B Bonds as Qualified Energy Conservation 
Bonds. 
Ordinary Optional Redemption with respect to the Series 2015B Bonds.   T h e  
Series 2015B Bonds maturing on or after December 1, 2025 are subject to redemption, by 
and at the sole option of the City, in whole or in part (in increm ents of $5,000, in such 
order of maturity as may be  determined by the City) on any Business Day, at the Make-
Whole Redemption Price (as defined below). 
Extraordinary Optional Redemption with respect to the Series 2015B Bonds.  The 
Series 2015B Bonds are subject to extraordin ary optional redemption prior to maturity, 
upon the occurrence of an event resulting in the reduction or cessation of the Qualified 
Energy Conservation Payments (as defined herein) related to the Series 2015B Bonds, by 
and at the sole option of the City, in whole or in part (in increm ents of $5,000, in such 
order of maturity as may be  determined by the City) on any Business Day, at the Make-
Whole Redemption Price, with any reduction  being measured against the Qualified 
Energy Conservation Payments in effect at the time of issuance of the Series 2015B 
Bonds, other than a reduction or cessation that  is the result of an act or omission by the 
City relative to the requirements of the Code and regulations thereunder.   
For purposes of this section, the terms listed below shall have the following 
meanings: 
“Business Day” means any day other than (i) a Saturday or a Sunday, (ii) 
a day on which the Paying Agent and Registra r is authorized to close or is not 
prohibited from closing, by law (includi ng, without limitation, executive orders), 
and is closed, (iii) any day on which th e Federal Reserve Ba nk of Cleveland is 
closed, or (iv) a day on which the securities depository is closed. 
“Make-Whole Adjustment” means (i) with respect to ordinary optional 
redemption, 30 basis points or (ii) with  respect to extr aordinary optional 
redemption, 100 basis points. 
“Make-Whole Redemption Price”  means the greater of (i) 100% of the 
principal amount of a Series  2015B Bond to be redeemed or (ii) the sum of the 
present value of the remaining scheduled payments of principal and interest to the 
maturity date of the Series 2015B Bonds to be redeemed (the “Scheduled 
Redemption Date”), not including any por tion of those payments of interest 
accrued and unpaid as of the date on whic h such Series 2015B Bond is to be 
redeemed, discounted to the Scheduled Redemption Date on a semi-annual basis, 
assuming a 360-day year consisting of tw elve 30-day months, at the adjusted 
Treasury Rate plus the Make-Whole Adjust ment, plus, in each case, accrued and 
unpaid interest on such Series 2015B Bond to the Scheduled Redemption Date. 
The City will retain an independent  accounting firm or an independent 
financial advisor to determine the Make-Whole Redemption Price and will 
perform all actions and make all calculations required to determine the Make-
Whole Redemption Price. The City may conc lusively rely on the calculations of 
such accounting firm or financial advi sor in connection with, and in its 
determination of, the Make-Whole Redempti on Price, and the City will not have  
13 
any liability for its reliance.  The de termination of the Make-Whole Redemption 
Price by such accounting firm or financial advisor shall be conclusive and binding 
on the Paying Agent and Registrar, the C ity and the owners of the Series 2015B 
Bonds.   
“Treasury Rate” means the yield to maturity, as of the redemption date of 
a Series 2015B Bond (or portion thereof), of United States Treasury securities 
with a constant maturity (as compiled a nd published in the most recent Federal 
Reserve Statistical Release H.15 (519) that  has become publicly available at least 
two Business Days prior to delivery of th e notice of redemption of such Series 
2015B Bond (or portion thereof) (excluding inflation indexed securities) (or, if 
such Statistical Release is no longer pub lished, any publicly available source of 
similar market data)) most nearly equal to the period from such redemption date 
to the maturity date of such Series 2015B Bond; provided, however, that if the 
period from such redemption date to such maturity date is less than one year, the 
weekly average yield on actually traded United States Treasury securities adjusted 
to a constant maturity of one year will be used. 
Extraordinary Mandatory Redemption for the Series 2015B Bonds.   The Series 
2015B Bonds are subject to extraordinary mandato ry redemption, in whole or in part at a 
redemption price of 100% of the principal amou nt to be redeemed, plus accrued interest 
to the redemption date, if the City fails to expend all of the available project proceeds (as 
defined in Section 54A of the Code) of th e Series 2015B Bonds within the three-year 
period beginning on the issue date of the Seri es 2015B Bonds (or, if an extension of the 
expenditure period has been received by the City from the Secretary of the U.S. Treasury, 
at the close of the extended period) within ni nety 90 days of the end of such period to the 
extent necessary, in the opinion of nationa lly recognized bond couns el rendered at the 
request of the City or pursuant to a final determination by the Internal Revenue Service or 
court of competent jurisdiction in the United St ates, to preserve the federal tax status of 
the Series 2015B Bonds as Qualified Energy Conservation Bonds. 
Sinking Fund for the Series 2015B Bonds .  The City will establish a sinking fund 
in connection with the Series 2015B Bonds (the “Series 2015B QECB Sinking Fund”), to 
be held by the Paying Agent and Registrar.  Pursuant to Section 54A(d)(4)(C) of the 
Code, a sinking fund may be established, provided that the following requirements are 
satisfied: (a) such fund is funded at a rate not  more rapid than equal annual installments; 
(b) such fund is funded in a manner reasonab ly expected to result in an amount not 
greater than an amount necessary to repay the Series 2015B Bonds; and (c) the yield on 
such fund is not greater than the discount rate determined by the Secretary of the U.S. 
Treasury under Section 54A(d)(5)( B) of the Code on the sale date of the Series 2015B 
Bonds, which was 3.08% (the “Permitted Sinking Fund Yield”).   
The City has covenanted to set aside certain funds each December 1, commencing 
December 1, 2016 and annually ther eafter until the maturity of the Series 2015B Bonds, 
which when combined with amounts ear ned pursuant to a Sinking Fund Forward 
Delivery Agreement, dated on or about the da te of delivery of the Series 2015B Bonds, 
will be applied to the paymen t of the principal amount of the Series 2015B Bonds at 
maturity.  Pursuant to such Forward Deli very Agreement, the amounts in the Series 
2015B QECB Sinking Fund shall be invested with a rate of return which will be less than 
the Permitted Sinking Fund Yield.

Redemption Procedures 
Selection of Bonds for Redemption.   When partial redemption of any Series 
2015A Bonds and/or Series 2015B Bonds is au thorized, the Bond Registrar will select 
such Series 2015A Bonds and/or Series 2015B Bonds or portions thereof by lot within a 
maturity in such manner as the Bond Registrar may determine, or as directed by the City, 
provided, however, that the portion of a ny Series 2015A Bonds and/or Series 2015B 
Bonds so selected will be in the amount of $5,000 or an integral multiple thereof. 
Notice of Redemption.  The notice of the call for redemption of any Series 2015A 
Bonds and/or Series 2015B Bonds shall identify (i) by de signation, letters, numbers, or 
other distinguishing marks the Series 2015A Bonds and/or the Series 2015B Bonds, or 
portions thereof, to be redeemed, (ii) the rede mption price to be pai d, (iii) the date fixed 
for redemption, and (iv) the place or places  where the amounts due upon redemption are 
payable.  From and after the specified re demption date and provided that funds are on 
hand and available for the payment of interest due as of the redemption date, interest on 
the respective Series 2015A Bonds and/or the Series 2015B Bonds (or portions thereof) 
called for redemption shall cease to accrue.  Su ch notice shall be sent by registered or 
certified mail at the address shown in th e Series 2015A Bonds and the Series 2015B 
Bonds registration records not less than 30 days nor more than 60 days prior to the date  
15 
of redemption.  Failure to receive such notice or any defect therein shall not affect the 
validity of the proceedings for the redemption of any Series 2015A Bonds and/or the 
Series 2015B Bonds. 
Book-Entry Only System 
The Series 2015 Bonds will be issued as B ook Entry Only Bonds.  The Depository Trust 
Company (“DTC”), New York, New York, will act as securities depository for the Series 2015 
Bonds.  The Series 2015 Bonds will be issued as fully-registered securities registered in the name 
of Cede & Co. (DTC's partners hip nominee).  One fully-register ed Series 2015 Bond certificate 
will be issued for each maturity of the same interest rate of the Series 2015 Bonds in the 
aggregate principal amount of such maturity and will be deposited with DTC.  See Appendix D 
hereto for further description of DTC. 
SECURITY AND SOURCE OF PAYMENT 
Unlimited Property Tax 
The Series 2015 Bonds are unvoted general obliga tion debt of the City.  The security for 
the Series 2015 Bonds is the City's ability to levy an ad valorem tax on all real and personal 
property in the City subject to ad valorem taxation by the City without limitation as to rate and 
amount unless paid from other sources.  (See Appendix A - “DEBT STRUCTURE - Ad Valorem 
Tax Base.”) 
A 1977 decision of the Ohio First District  Court of Appeals, which the Supreme 
Court of Ohio declined to review, upheld the right of the City, in accordance with the City's 
Charter, to levy property taxes without limit ation in excess of the ten-mill limitation 
contained in Section 5705.02 of the Ohio Revised Code to support its lawfully issued bonds 
and notes and established that the City's abilit y to incur debt will be limited only by the 
arithmetical (percentage) limitations contained in Section 133.05 of the Ohio Revised Code. 
(See Appendix A  - “DEBT STRUCTURE - Applicab le Debt Limitations” in Appendix A  
hereto.) 
Full Faith and Credit 
The ordinances authorizing the issuance of  the Series 2015 Bonds provide further 
security by making a general pled ge of the full faith and credit of the City for the payment of 
principal and interest on the Series 2015 Bonds as the same become due.  Included in such 
general pledge are all funds of  the City except those specifically limited to another use or 
prohibited from use for such debt service by the Oh io Constitution or Ohio or Federal laws such 
as tax levies voted for specific purposes and taxes levied for de bt service on specific voted 
general obligation bond issues. 
  
16

Payments 
Principal and semi-annual interest on the Series 2015 Bonds will be paid to the Registrar.  
So long as DTC or its nominee, Cede & Co., is the bondholder, such paymen ts will be made to 
Cede & Co., which in turn will remit such payments to the DTC Direct Participants (as defined 
in Appendix D hereto) and DTC Indirect Participants (as defined in Appendix D hereto) for 
subsequent disbursement to the Beneficial Owners (as defined in Appendix D hereto) of the 
Series 2015 Bonds. Interest will be payable on December 1, 2015 and semiannually thereafter on 
each June 1 and December 1 until final maturit y.  Principal will be payable annually on the 
Series 2015 Bonds (either upon schedul ed maturity or mandatory redemption) on the first day of 
December in the years and amounts as provided on th e inside cover page he reof for each series, 
until final maturity.  Upon receipt of principal or interest payments, DTC's current practice is to 
credit immediately the accounts of Participants (as defined in Appendix D hereto) in accordance 
with their respective positions as shown on DTC's records.

USE OF BOND PROCEEDS 
Use of Proceeds of the Series 2015A Bonds 
Series 2015A Improvement Bonds.  The proceeds of the Series 2015A Improvement 
Bonds are being issued for the purpose of providing funds in the following amounts and for the 
following projects: 
(a) $8,355,000 Street Improvement Bonds, Seri es A, Maturing December 
1, 2035: Proceeds to be used for  street improvements in cluding widening, opening, 
extending, realigning, grading, repaving, resurfacing, constructing sewers and drains or 
otherwise rehabilitating and improving st reets, roads, thoroughfares, avenues, 
expressways, sidewalks, plazas and other public  ways; purchasing and/or installing street 
lights and equipment and boulevard lights; refunding related bond anticipation notes in 
the principal amount of $8,030,000; and payi ng legal advertising, printing and all 
expenses incidental to said improvements.  Such Street Improvement Bonds, Series A 
will be issued as Series 2015A Bonds. 
(b) $1,920,000 Public Buildings Improvement Bonds, Series A, Maturing 
December 1, 2030: Proceeds to be used for acquiring real estate or interests in same, 
constructing, rehabilitating and equipping public buildings and other structures; refunding 
related bond anticipation notes  in the principal amount of  $247,650; and paying legal, 
advertising, printing and all expenses incide ntal to said improvements.  Such Public 
Buildings Improvement Bonds, Series A will be issued as Series 2015A Bonds. 
(c) $4,725,000 Equipment Improvement Bonds, Maturing December 1, 
2020: Proceeds to be used for  purchasing motor vehicles, acquiring and improving City-
wide communication system components and related improvements, computer related 
system upgrades and components and other necessary equipment; refunding related bond 
anticipation notes in the pr incipal amount of $4,406,430; and pa ying legal, advertising, 
printing and all expenses incidental to  said improvements.  Such Equipment 
Improvement Bonds will be issued as Series 2015A Bonds. 
(d) $4,000,000 Parks and Recreation Improvement Bonds, Maturing 
December 1, 2030:  Proceeds to be used for the re funding of bond anticipation notes in 
the principal amount of $4,000,000, the proceeds of which were used for park 
improvements including acquiring real estate or  interests in same, for parks, parkways, 
playgrounds and recreation centers, improving a nd equipping such real estate and also 
rehabilitating existing parks, parkways, play grounds and recreation centers, and paying 
legal, advertising, printing and all expenses in cidental to said improvements.  Such Parks 
and Recreation Improvement Bonds will be issued as Series 2015A Bonds.  
(e) $4,000,000 Street Improvement Bonds, Maturing December 1, 2035:  
Proceeds to be used for the refunding of bond anticipation notes in the principal amount 
of $4,000,000, the proceeds of which were used for widening, opening, extending, 
realigning, grading, repaving, resurfacing, constr ucting sewers and drains or otherwise 
rehabilitating and improving streets, road s, thoroughfares, avenues, expressways. 
sidewalks, plazas and other public ways; purch asing and/or installing street lights and 
equipment and boulevard lights, and paying lega l, advertising, printing, and all expenses  
19 
incidental to said improvements.  Such Street Improvement Bonds will be issued as 
Series 2015A Bonds. 
(f) $3,000,000 Public Building Improvement Bonds, Maturing December 
1, 2030: Proceeds to be used for ac quiring real estate or inte rests in same, constructing, 
rehabilitating and equipping public buildings  and other structures  and paying legal, 
advertising, printing and all expenses incide ntal to said improvements.  Such Public 
Buildings Improvement Bonds will be issued as Series 2015A Bonds. 
(g) $7,500,000 Public Building Improvement Bonds (Linn Street Facility), 
Maturing December 1, 2040: Proceeds to be used for the refunding of bond anticipation 
notes in the principal amount of $7,500,000, the proceeds of which were used for various 
improvements associated with public buildin g improvements includ ing acquiring real 
estate or interests in same, constructing, rehabilitating and equipping public buildings and 
other structures (collectively, the “Linn Street Facility Project”) and paying legal, 
advertising, printing, and all expenses incidental to sa id improvements.  Such Public 
Buildings Improvement Bonds (Linn Street F acility) will be issu ed as Series 2015A 
Bonds. 
(h) $6,000,000 Economic Development Revenue Bonds (VP3 Parking 
Garage), Maturing December 1, 2035:  Proceeds to be used for the refunding of bond 
anticipation notes in the principal amount  of $6,000,000, the proceeds of which were 
used for paying a portion of the City’s share of  the costs associated with the construction 
of a new parking garage in the Corryville Neighborhood Business District of Cincinnati 
and paying legal, advertising, pr inting, and all expenses incide ntal to said improvements.  
Such Economic Development Revenue Bonds (V P3 Parking Garage) will be issued as 
Series 2015A Bonds. 
(i) $3,175,000 Public Building Improvement Bonds (Energy Management 
Building Project), Maturing December 1, 2035: Proceeds to be used for the refunding 
of a portion in the principal amount of $3,175,000 of bond anticipation notes (issued in 
the total principal amount of $3,550,000), the pr oceeds of which were used for acquiring, 
installing and servicing energy cost-savings  equipment and energy management building 
improvements for the purpose of achieving energy cost reductions and cost savings 
within various public buildings and paying le gal advertising, printi ng and all expenses 
incidental to said improvements.  Such Public Building Improvement Bonds (Energy 
Management Building Project) will be issued as Series 2015A Bonds.   
(j) $1,160,000 Public Building Improvement Bonds, Maturing December 
1, 2035: Proceeds to be used for the refunding of  bond anticipation notes in the principal 
amount of $1,160,000, the proceeds of which were used for purchasing, installing and 
servicing energy cost savings equipment and related equipment within the Duke Energy 
Convention Center and to provide other services for the purpose of achieving energy cost 
reductions within the Duke Energy Conventi on Center and paying legal advertising, 
printing and all expenses incidental to sa id improvements.  Such Public Building 
Improvement Bonds will be issued as Series 2015A Bonds. 
(k) $25,000,000 Urban Redevelopment Bonds (Streetcar System), 
Maturing December 1, 2040 : Proceeds to be used for reimbursing the City for monies 
previously spent for improvements includi ng, certain urban redevelopment activities  
20 
associated with the Streetcar Project incl uding but not limited to, planning and design 
work, streetcar vehicles, other rolling stock, tracks, electrification system, catenaries, 
equipment, maintenance and storage facilities, infrastructure improvements and refunding 
bond anticipation notes previously issued for the purpose of financing such 
improvements related to the Streetcar Project (collectively, the “Streetcar Project”), and 
paying legal advertising, printing and all expenses incidental to said improvements.  Such 
Streetcar System Project Bonds will be issued as Series 2015A Bonds. 
Series 2015A Refunding Bonds.   The proceeds of the Se ries 2015A Refunding Bonds 
will be used for the purpose of (i) refunding th e Refunded Tax-Exempt Bonds (detailed below) 
and (ii) paying legal, advertisi ng, printing, and all expenses inci dental to the issuance of the 
Series 2015A Refunding Bonds. 
The net proceeds of the Series 2015A Refunding  Bonds will be applied to the refunding 
of the Refunded Tax-Exempt Bonds, with the result that the Refunded Tax-Exempt Bonds will 
be deemed to be paid and no longer outstanding.

The net proceeds of the Series 2015A Re funding Bonds will be deposited in an 
irrevocable escrow account (the “Refunded Tax-Exempt Bonds Escr ow Account”) to be held by 
an escrow trustee pursuant to an escrow agreement between the City and such escrow trustee (the 
“Refunded Tax-Exempt Bonds Escrow Agreement” ).  Such proceeds will be used immediately 
to purchase direct obligations of , or obligations the principal of and interest on which are 
unconditionally guaranteed by, the United States of America, or other obligations the principal of 
and interest on which are fully secured by the foregoing in such amounts and maturities, and 
bearing interest at rates, so as to provide suffi cient moneys to pay when due certain maturities of 
the principal and interest due on the Refunded Tax-Exempt Bonds. 
Use of Proceeds of the Series 2015B Bonds 
The Series 2015B Bonds are be ing issued for the purpose of providing funds in the 
following amount and for the following project: 
$3,450,000 Energy Conservation General Obligation Bonds, Series 2015B 
(Federally Taxable -Qualified Energy Conservation Bond – Direct Payment to the 
Issuer), Maturing December 1, 2035:  Proceeds to be used for the refunding of bond 
anticipation notes in the principal amount  of $3,450,000, the proceeds of which were 
used for acquiring, installing and servicing energy cost-savings equipment and energy 
management building improvements for the purpose of achieving energy cost reductions 
and cost savings within various public bui ldings and paying legal advertising, printing 
and all expenses incidental to said improvements.  Such Energy Conservation General 
Obligation Bonds, Series 2015B (Federally  Taxable -Qualified Energy Conservation 
Bond – Direct Payment to the Issuer) (ori ginally designated as Energy Management 
Building Improvement Bonds) will be issued as Series 2015B Bonds.

RATINGS 
As noted on the cover page of this Offi cial Statement, Moody's Investors Service 
(“Moody’s”) and Standard & Poor's Ratings Services, a division of The McGraw-Hill 
Companies, Inc. (“Standard & Poor's”), have assigned ratings to the Series 2015 Bonds of “Aa2” 
(stable outlook) and “ AA-” (stable outlook), respectively.  Such ratings reflect only the 
respective views of such organizations and an explanation of the significance of such ratings may 
be obtained from the rating agency furnishing the same. 
The City has supplied and expects to provi de the rating agencies with additional 
information that may not be included herein.  Th e City can provide no assu rance that the ratings 
accorded the Series 2015 Bonds will continue for any given period of time or that the ratings will 
not be revised upward, downward, or withdrawn entir ely by either or both rating agencies, if, in 
the judgment of either or both organizations, ci rcumstances so warrant.  Any downward revision 
or withdrawal of such ratings may have an adverse effect on the market price of the Series 2015 
Bonds.

UNDERWRITING 
The Underwriters have agreed, subject to cer tain conditions, to purchase the Series 2015 
Bonds from the City as follows: 
(a) The Series 2015A Bonds at a pur chase price of $139,964,625.88, which is 
equal to the par amount of the Series 2015A Bonds of $122,530,000.00, plus net 
premium of $17,995,605.30, less underwriters' discount of $560,979.42.  The net amount 
received by the City will be $139,264,612.79, wh ich is equal to th e purchase price of 
$139,964,625.88, less $700,013.09, representing the costs of issuance to be retained by 
the Underwriters to be paid on behalf of the City.  Excess premium in the amount of 
$8,503,485.69 shall be deposited into the City's bond retirement fund;  
(b) The Series 2015B Bonds from the City at a purchase price of 
$3,431,236.78, which is equal to the par amount  of the Series 2015B Bonds of 
$3,450,000.00, less underwriters' discount of $18,763.22.  The net amount received by 
the City will be $3,411,565.52, which is e qual to the purchase price of $3,431,236.78, 
less $19,671.26, representing the cost s of issuance to be  retained by the Underwriters to 
be paid on behalf of the City; and 
(c) The Series 2015C Refunding Bonds from the City at a purchase price of 
$14,639,854.22, which is equal to the par amount of the Series 2015C Refunding Bonds 
of $14,690,000.00, less underwriters' discount of $50,145.78.  The net amount received 
by the City will be $14,554,143.79, which is equal to the purchase price of 
$14,639,854.22, less $85,710.43, representing the costs of is suance to be retained by the 
Underwriters to be paid on behalf of the City.   
The Underwriters are obligated to purchase all of the Series 2015 Bonds, pursuant to the 
Bond Purchase Agreement, dated August 6, 2015.  The Series 2015 Bonds may be offered and 
sold by the Underwriters to certai n dealers at prices lower than the public offering price, and the 
public offering price may be changed from time to time. 
The Underwriters and their respective affiliates are full-service financial institutions 
engaged in various activities th at may include securities tradi ng, commercial and investment 
banking, municipal advisory, broker age, and asset management.  In the ordinary course of 
business, the Underwriters and their respecti ve affiliates may actively trade debt and, if 
applicable, equity securities (or related deriva tive securities) and provide financial instruments 
(which may include bank loans, cr edit support or interest rate swaps).  The Underwriters and 
their respective affiliates ma y engage in transactions fo r their own accounts involving the 
securities and instruments made the subject of this securities o ffering or other offering of the 
Issuer.  The Underwriters and th eir respective affiliates may make  a market in credit default 
swaps with respect to municipal se curities in the future.  The U nderwriters and their respective 
affiliates may also communicate independent i nvestment recommendations, market color or 
trading ideas and publish independent research views in respect of  this securities offering or 
other offerings of the Issuer.
